lines: A child drew here in white chalk, low on the stone where a small person kneels: a round, ringed world with a long line falling out of the bottom of it, and a little stick figure looking up at the fall.
Under it, in shaky letters no grown hand would form: THE COLD WHITE MOON. IT IS WAKING UP.

No one in this parish has ever seen a moon like that, and no child here was taught to draw one. They drew it anyway. The wardens say children have started drawing the same cold moon in clean cities that never burned. No one can say what it means, and the Censure does not encourage the asking.
